pages.full_text: the organ here. The house is like Cromwell's house at Ely. Low ceilings, wooden, cosy. After we had looked around a woman played a pipe organ used for [?continuo], a clavichord (quick) ~~a harpsichord~~, a spinet (small harpsichord)
pages.notes: Page has a glued-in photo of a yellow building (Bach's House, Eisenach) in snow. Vertical marginalia on left reads 'Musical instruments / Bach's House / Eisenach'. Text is mid-sentence continuing from a previous page. The word used after 'pipe organ used for' could be 'continuo' or 'continue'. '~~a harpsichord~~' appears struck through with a line. No handwritten page number visible in top-left corner. Right edge of text is partially cut off by gutter/overlap.

pages.full_text:
+ then an organ, Thomas pulled straps to pump it.
	We drove on to a campsite by a lake 1km south of __Eisenach__ *44. Charles + Eve swam across the lake. Eve did 2 washes but unfortunately one of the machines was only a drier, so it had to be rewashed in the otter machine later. Thomas is doing the shopping. Good round black pudding (sweet? smoked?).
	Simon + Eve slept badly from having eaten too much!
	__Wednesday__ (St. Elisabeth of Hungary + more)
We arrived at __Wartburg Castle__ + waited ages for a guided tour. The speaker spoke in German, while English people were handed notes. It was a very modern castle, 1160s with some underfloor heating + latrines. lovely carved bases to columns. Much restored in [?C9] + parts reminded us of Arts + Crafts + Pre Raphaelites. musical instruments
Luther translated the New Testament here in 10 weeks when banished from empire 1521 ([?King Friedrich] [?the wise] hid him as 'Junker [illegible]', [illegible] [illegible] Duke of Saxony
pages.notes: Page contains two glued-in photos: a portrait labelled 'J S Bach' and a small photo of carved musical instruments in the bottom-right corner, captioned 'musical instruments'. Top-right page number corner appears to show '8' but is unclear — could be part of a larger number or a page reference. The reference '*44' after Eisenach likely refers to a campsite page cross-reference. 'otter machine' may be 'other machine' (handwriting reading). The final lines about Luther/1521 are partially cut off at page bottom; names after 'Junker' are illegible. 'C9' may refer to the 19th century (C19). iso_guess for Wednesday is null as the year cannot be confirmed from this page alone, though volume context suggests late 1990s or early 2000s.
